Description:
Goal:
Create the agent-facing Git workflow tools under tools/agent/ so every slice can be tested, committed, logged, and pushed.

Implementation notes:
- Use tools/agent/git-init.sh, git-slice.sh, git-status.sh.
- Expose npm scripts: git:init, git:status, git:slice.
- Add .cline/rules/git-workflow.md.

Acceptance criteria:
- npm run git:init works.
- npm run git:status works.
- git:slice runs tests before committing.
- docs/SLICE_LOG.md is updated.
- No secrets/node_modules/build artifacts are committed.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "create repo-local git slice workflow" "npm run git:status"

Goal:
Confirm the assignment structure, uploaded CSVs, tests, required port, and source-code constraints before implementation.

Implementation notes:
- Do not modify tests/ or data/.
- Capture any README/test mismatch in docs/IMPLEMENTATION_NOTES.md.

Acceptance criteria:
- Confirm backend/frontend code lives under src/.
- Confirm API base is /api.
- Confirm server listens on port 3000.
- Confirm tests are authoritative when they disagree with README.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "validate assignment files and constraints" "manual"

Goal:
Create the initial Node.js TypeScript backend and React TypeScript frontend structure under src/.

Implementation notes:
- Keep structure simple and assignment-focused.
- Do not implement business features in this card.

Acceptance criteria:
- Backend entrypoint exists.
- Frontend app shell exists.
- Shared types/config folders exist if useful.
- Root scripts exist for dev/build/start/test helpers.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "create project skeleton" "npm run build"

Goal:
Create safe config loading for PostgreSQL, Redis, server port, and runtime mode.

Implementation notes:
- Default DB should match assignment: localhost:5432 postgres/postgres order_ops.
- Default Redis should match localhost:6379.
- .env.example only, never real .env.

Acceptance criteria:
- .env.example exists.
- Config can be overridden by environment variables.
- Missing optional Redis does not crash basic API if not required.
- .env is ignored by Git.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "configure environment and safe defaults" "npm run build"

Goal:
Add root scripts that run assignment tests from the tests folder when it exists.

Implementation notes:
- Scripts should fail clearly if tests/ is missing.

Acceptance criteria:
- Can run basic tests from root.
- Can run filter/agg/anomaly/bulk/concurrent/perf/realtime/security tests.
- Can run full grade command if available.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "add root test runner shortcuts" "npm run test:basic"

Card Title: Explore CSV data and edge cases
Description:
Goal:
Analyze the CSV files before final schema/import design.

Implementation notes:
- Inspect orders, suppliers, products, categories.
- Generate a short docs/DATA_NOTES.md.

Acceptance criteria:
- Record row counts: 50,000 orders, 500 suppliers, 5,000 products, 195 categories.
- Identify null/empty warehouses.
- Identify negative quantities.
- Identify price mismatches.
- Identify inactive suppliers with orders.
- Identify timestamp anomalies.
- Identify circular categories.
- Identify XSS-like notes.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "explore csv data and edge cases" "npm run data:inspect"

Goal:
Create database schema for suppliers, categories, products, orders, jobs, and job_items.

Implementation notes:
- Use text IDs from CSVs as primary keys.
- Add version/lock field to orders for optimistic concurrency.
- Keep schema practical for tests and performance.

Acceptance criteria:
- Suppliers table supports active/rating/country.
- Categories supports parent_id.
- Products references category_id.
- Orders references supplier_id and product_id.
- Jobs and job_items support bulk progress.
- Schema can be recreated from scratch.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "design postgresql schema" "npm run db:migrate"

Goal:
Implement migration scripts for all tables and performance indexes.

Implementation notes:
- Indexes should support filtering, sorting, joins, aggregations, anomaly checks, and job polling.

Acceptance criteria:
- Indexes exist for orders status, priority, supplier_id, warehouse, created_at, total_price, product_id.
- Indexes exist for products category_id and lower/name search strategy.
- Indexes exist for jobs/job_items status and job_id.
- Migrations are repeatable or have a safe reset path.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "create migrations and indexes" "npm run db:migrate"

Goal:
Import all CSV seed data into PostgreSQL.

Implementation notes:
- Handle empty strings/nulls.
- Parse booleans/numbers/timestamps safely.
- Use batch inserts or COPY for speed.

Acceptance criteria:
- Imports all 4 CSVs.
- Final counts match expected counts.
- Import can reset and rerun cleanly.
- No malformed rows silently disappear.
- Import preserves notes as strings.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "implement csv import pipeline" "npm run data:import"

Card Title: Implement recursive category lookup with cycle guard
Description:
Goal:
Support product filtering by category including all descendant categories, while avoiding infinite loops from circular data.

Implementation notes:
- Can use recursive CTE with cycle protection or app-level traversal with visited set.

Acceptance criteria:
- GET /api/products?category=cat_001 includes child-category products.
- Circular category data cannot hang/crash.
- Category filter remains performant.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "implement recursive category lookup with cycle guard" "npm run test:basic"

Goal:
Create DB pool, query helper, transaction helper, and repository pattern.

Implementation notes:
- Use parameterized queries only.
- Make transactions easy for PATCH and bulk jobs.

Acceptance criteria:
- DB helper centralizes connection handling.
- Repository methods are typed.
- Transaction helper supports commit/rollback.
- No SQL interpolation for user values.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "create database access layer" "npm run build"

Goal:
Create the HTTP server and /api route structure.

Implementation notes:
- Use Express/Fastify or a simple Node framework.
- All responses should be JSON for API endpoints.

Acceptance criteria:
- Server listens on port 3000 by default.
- Unknown routes return 404 JSON.
- Content-Type is application/json for API responses.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "bootstrap backend server on port 3000" "npm run test:basic"

Goal:
Standardize paginated list responses and error responses.

Implementation notes:
- List response shape: { data, total, limit, offset }.
- Error shape: { error, code } where possible.

Acceptance criteria:
- All list endpoints use the same pagination shape.
- 404 responses include string error.
- 400/409 responses include string error.
- Content-Type is application/json.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "create response and error helpers" "npm run test:basic"

Goal:
Return paginated orders.

Implementation notes:
- Default limit is 20.
- Support limit and offset.
- Return total count across all orders.

Acceptance criteria:
- GET /api/orders returns 200.
- data is an array.
- default data length <= 20.
- total equals 50,000.
- limit=50&offset=100 returns 50 rows.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "implement get /api/orders" "npm run test:basic"

Goal:
Return a single order by ID with joined supplier and product names.

Implementation notes:
- Join suppliers and products.
- Keep notes/status/priority fields intact.

Acceptance criteria:
- Existing order returns 200.
- Response includes supplier_name.
- Response includes product_name.
- Missing order returns 404.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "implement get /api/orders/:id" "npm run test:basic"

Goal:
Update order fields safely.

Implementation notes:
- Support status, priority, notes.
- Reject invalid status.
- Reject updates to cancelled orders.
- Prepare for optimistic locking.

Acceptance criteria:
- Valid status update returns 200.
- Updated status is returned.
- Invalid status returns 400.
- Cancelled order update returns 409.
- Status changes publish order_updated hook if realtime is already present.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "implement patch /api/orders/:id" "npm run test:basic"

Goal:
Return paginated suppliers.

Implementation notes:
- Support limit and offset using shared helper.

Acceptance criteria:
- GET /api/suppliers returns 200.
- data is an array.
- total equals 500.
- Content-Type is application/json.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "implement get /api/suppliers" "npm run test:basic"

Goal:
Return a supplier with computed order_count and total_revenue.

Implementation notes:
- Use SQL aggregation over orders.
- Return zero values safely if no orders.

Acceptance criteria:
- sup_042 returns 200.
- Response includes order_count.
- Response includes total_revenue.
- Missing supplier returns 404.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "implement get /api/suppliers/:id" "npm run test:basic"

Goal:
Return paginated products and support category recursive filtering.

Implementation notes:
- Use recursive category lookup card if already complete.

Acceptance criteria:
- GET /api/products returns 200.
- total equals 5,000.
- GET /api/products?category=cat_001 returns products across more than one category.
- Content-Type is application/json.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "implement get /api/products" "npm run test:basic"

Goal:
Run and fix the full basic CRUD test suite.

Implementation notes:
- Do not move on until this is stable unless blocked by assignment constraints.

Acceptance criteria:
- All basic CRUD tests pass.
- Commit and push the slice.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "pass basic crud suite" "npm run test:basic"

Card Title: Implement order query parser and validation
Description:
Goal:
Create a safe parser for /api/orders query parameters.

Implementation notes:
- Parse status, priority, supplier_id, warehouse, date_from, date_to, min_total, search, sort, order, limit, offset.
- Use allowlists for sort/order.

Acceptance criteria:
- Invalid sort fields cannot inject SQL.
- order only allows asc/desc.
- limit is validated or clamped.
- Parsed query object is typed.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "implement order query parser and validation" "npm run test:filter"

Goal:
Support status and priority filtering on GET /api/orders.

Implementation notes:
- Status supports single value and comma-separated values.

Acceptance criteria:
- ?status=pending works.
- ?status=pending,approved works.
- ?priority=critical works.
- Filters combine correctly.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "implement status and priority filters" "npm run test:filter"

Goal:
Support supplier_id and warehouse filters on GET /api/orders.

Implementation notes:
- Use exact matching.
- Warehouse may contain values like warehouse_east.

Acceptance criteria:
- ?supplier_id=sup_042 works.
- ?warehouse=warehouse_east works.
- Filters combine with status/priority.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "implement supplier and warehouse filters" "npm run test:filter"

Card Title: Implement date range and min_total filters
Description:
Goal:
Support created_at date range and minimum total filters.

Implementation notes:
- date_from/date_to should be inclusive.
- min_total means total_price >= value.

Acceptance criteria:
- date_from works.
- date_to works inclusively.
- date range works together.
- min_total works.
- Invalid numeric/date input is handled safely.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "implement date range and min_total filters" "npm run test:filter"

Goal:
Support case-insensitive search on joined product name.

Implementation notes:
- Search applies to product_name.
- Use parameterized query.
- Consider lower(name) index or pg_trgm if available.

Acceptance criteria:
- ?search=hydraulic returns matching order rows.
- Search combines with other filters.
- No SQL injection risk.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "implement product-name search" "npm run test:filter"

Goal:
Support allowlisted sorting and direction on GET /api/orders.

Implementation notes:
- Sort by fields expected by tests such as total_price and created_at.
- Do not allow arbitrary SQL field strings.

Acceptance criteria:
- sort=total_price&order=desc works.
- sort=created_at&order=asc works.
- Default sort is deterministic.
- Invalid sort/order cannot break the query.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "implement sorting" "npm run test:filter"

Card Title: Verify combined filters and pagination totals
Description:
Goal:
Ensure all filters combine freely and pagination shape remains correct.

Implementation notes:
- Use SQL builder that composes WHERE clauses safely.

Acceptance criteria:
- status + priority + sort + order + limit works.
- Filtered total reflects all filters.
- data length respects limit.
- offset works with filters.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "verify combined filters and pagination totals" "npm run test:filter"

Goal:
Run and fix the full filtering test suite.

Implementation notes:
- Keep basic CRUD passing.

Acceptance criteria:
- All filtering tests pass.
- Basic CRUD still passes.
- Commit and push the slice.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "pass filtering suite" "npm run test:filter"

Goal:
Return dashboard-level total_orders, total_revenue, and avg_order_value.

Implementation notes:
- Calculate from all orders.
- Use numeric precision suitable for tests.

Acceptance criteria:
- total_orders equals 50,000.
- total_revenue is accurate.
- avg_order_value is accurate.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "implement get /api/orders/stats base totals" "npm run test:agg"

Goal:
Add status distribution to /api/orders/stats.

Implementation notes:
- Include count and total_value per status.
- Include all valid statuses even if count is 0.

Acceptance criteria:
- pending/approved/rejected/shipped/delivered/cancelled keys exist.
- Each has count and total_value.
- Values are accurate.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "implement by_status aggregation" "npm run test:agg"

Goal:
Add monthly order count and revenue trend to /api/orders/stats.

Implementation notes:
- Format month as YYYY-MM.
- Data spans 2023-01 through 2024-12.

Acceptance criteria:
- Exactly 24 entries.
- Sorted chronologically.
- Each entry has month, order_count, revenue.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "implement by_month aggregation" "npm run test:agg"

Goal:
Add top suppliers by revenue to /api/orders/stats.

Implementation notes:
- Join suppliers for names.
- Sort descending by total_revenue.

Acceptance criteria:
- Exactly 10 entries.
- Each has supplier_id, supplier_name, total_revenue.
- Sorted descending.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "implement top_suppliers aggregation" "npm run test:agg"

Goal:
Add warehouse distribution to /api/orders/stats.

Implementation notes:
- Treat null or empty warehouse as unassigned.

Acceptance criteria:
- Includes 5 warehouses plus unassigned if present.
- Each entry has warehouse, count, total_value.
- Unassigned includes null/empty warehouses.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "implement by_warehouse aggregation" "npm run test:agg"

Goal:
Implement GET /api/suppliers/:id/performance.

Implementation notes:
- avg_delivery_days: delivered orders updated_at-created_at days.
- rejection_rate: rejected/total.
- avg_order_value: avg total_price.
- monthly_trend: per month order count.
- price_consistency: unit_price within 20% of product base price.

Acceptance criteria:
- Endpoint returns 200 for valid supplier.
- All required fields exist.
- monthly_trend uses YYYY-MM.
- price_consistency is 0..1.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "implement supplier performance endpoint" "npm run test:agg"

Goal:
Make aggregations performant enough for 50,000 orders.

Implementation notes:
- Use SQL grouping, indexes, and optional Redis/cache if needed.
- Avoid heavy app-side loops where SQL can aggregate.

Acceptance criteria:
- /api/orders/stats p95 under 500ms.
- /api/suppliers/sup_042/performance p95 under 500ms.
- No stale cache after updates if caching is used.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "optimize aggregation queries" "npm run test:perf"

Goal:
Run and fix the full aggregation test suite.

Implementation notes:
- Keep basic and filtering passing.

Acceptance criteria:
- All aggregation tests pass.
- Commit and push the slice.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "pass aggregation suite" "npm run test:agg"

Goal:
Implement GET /api/orders/anomalies with required data-quality rules.

Implementation notes:
- Required rules: price_mismatch, inactive_supplier, negative_quantity, timestamp_anomaly.

Acceptance criteria:
- Returns { data: [...] }.
- Each anomaly has order_id.
- anomaly_types is a non-empty string array.
- severity is low/medium/high.
- Orders can have multiple anomaly types.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "implement required anomaly rules" "npm run test:anomaly"

Goal:
Flag orders where total_price differs from quantity * unit_price by more than 0.01.

Implementation notes:
- Use absolute difference.
- Be careful with numeric precision.

Acceptance criteria:
- Sample expected price mismatch IDs are included.
- Rule name includes price_mismatch or price-like text.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "implement price mismatch anomaly" "npm run test:anomaly"

Goal:
Flag orders belonging to inactive suppliers.

Implementation notes:
- Join suppliers table.
- active false must be detected reliably.

Acceptance criteria:
- Sample inactive supplier order IDs are included.
- Rule name includes inactive_supplier.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "implement inactive supplier anomaly" "npm run test:anomaly"

Card Title: Implement negative quantity and timestamp anomalies
Description:
Goal:
Flag returns/negative quantities and impossible timestamps.

Implementation notes:
- negative_quantity: quantity < 0.
- timestamp_anomaly: updated_at < created_at.

Acceptance criteria:
- Sample negative quantity IDs are included.
- At least 80% of expected timestamp anomalies are flagged.
- Rule names are test-friendly.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "implement negative quantity and timestamp anomalies" "npm run test:anomaly"

Goal:
Add bonus anomaly types for price_spike, after_hours, and risky_supplier.

Implementation notes:
- price_spike: unit_price > 3x product base price.
- after_hours: created_at outside business hours like 22:00-06:00 UTC.
- risky_supplier: supplier has unusually high anomalous order rate.

Acceptance criteria:
- At least one price_spike is flagged.
- At least one after_hours is flagged.
- Risky supplier rule does not explode result size unreasonably.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "implement bonus anomaly rules" "npm run test:anomaly"

Goal:
Classify anomalies as low, medium, or high.

Implementation notes:
- High for negative quantity, timestamp anomaly, or multiple anomalies.
- Medium for price mismatch/inactive supplier.
- Low for softer bonus rules if alone.

Acceptance criteria:
- Every anomaly has valid severity.
- Severity rules are documented for ANOMALY_STRATEGY.md.
- Severity logic is deterministic.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "implement anomaly severity logic" "npm run test:anomaly"

Goal:
Make anomaly detection fast enough for the performance budget.

Implementation notes:
- Prefer SQL computation or efficient indexed joins.
- Avoid repeated per-row DB calls.

Acceptance criteria:
- /api/orders/anomalies p95 under 1000ms.
- Result shape remains correct.
- Required sample anomalies still appear.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "optimize anomaly endpoint" "npm run test:perf"

Goal:
Run and fix the full anomaly test suite.

Implementation notes:
- Keep prior suites passing.

Acceptance criteria:
- All anomaly tests pass.
- Commit and push the slice.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "pass anomaly suite" "npm run test:anomaly"

Goal:
Implement persistent job tracking for async bulk actions.

Implementation notes:
- Jobs track status and progress.
- Job items track per-order result when useful.

Acceptance criteria:
- Job statuses include processing, completed, failed.
- Progress includes total, completed, failed.
- Job can be polled immediately after creation.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "create jobs and job_items persistence" "npm run test:bulk"

Card Title: Implement bulk endpoint aliases and payload variants
Description:
Goal:
Support the README endpoint and test endpoint variants for bulk actions.

Implementation notes:
- Support /api/orders/bulk-action.
- Support /api/orders/bulk.
- Support /api/orders/bulk-actions.
- Accept orderIds and order_ids.
- Return jobId and job_id when helpful.

Acceptance criteria:
- POST /api/orders/bulk-action returns 202 with jobId.
- POST /api/orders/bulk works for security tests.
- POST /api/orders/bulk-actions works for concurrency tests.
- Invalid action returns 400.
- Empty IDs return 400.
- More than 10,000 IDs returns 400.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "implement bulk endpoint aliases and payload variants" "npm run test:bulk"

Goal:
Make bulk POST return immediately and process work in background.

Implementation notes:
- Use in-process worker loop, Redis queue, or lightweight DB-backed queue.
- For this assignment, simplest reliable background worker is acceptable.

Acceptance criteria:
- Bulk POST responds under 500ms.
- 1000-order submission responds under 500ms.
- API remains responsive while job runs.
- 1000-order job completes within 30 seconds when enough pending orders exist.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "implement async bulk processing" "npm run test:bulk"

Goal:
Apply valid bulk actions to selected orders.

Implementation notes:
- approve -> approved.
- reject -> rejected.
- flag can store a flagged marker/reason without corrupting status.
- Do not update cancelled orders.

Acceptance criteria:
- Valid orders are completed.
- Missing IDs are failed.
- Cancelled orders are failed/skipped.
- Reason field is accepted and stored/logged if useful.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "implement approve/reject/flag actions" "npm run test:bulk"

Goal:
Return job status and progress for polling.

Implementation notes:
- Return status and progress object.
- Use both jobId/job_id compatibility where needed elsewhere.

Acceptance criteria:
- Valid job returns 200.
- Response has status.
- Response has progress.total/completed/failed.
- Missing job returns 404.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "implement get /api/jobs/:id" "npm run test:bulk"

Goal:
Publish a bulk_completed event when a bulk job finishes.

Implementation notes:
- If realtime endpoint is not implemented yet, create EventPublisher interface/hook for later.

Acceptance criteria:
- Event type is bulk_completed.
- Data includes jobId.
- Hook is called exactly when job transitions to completed/failed.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "emit bulk_completed hook" "npm run test:realtime"

Goal:
Run and fix the full bulk operations test suite.

Implementation notes:
- Keep previous suites passing.

Acceptance criteria:
- All bulk tests pass.
- Commit and push the slice.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "pass bulk operations suite" "npm run test:bulk"

Goal:
Ensure two simultaneous PATCH requests to the same order produce one 200 and one 409.

Implementation notes:
- Use version column or atomic conditional update.
- Do not rely only on app memory.

Acceptance criteria:
- One concurrent PATCH succeeds.
- The other concurrent PATCH returns 409.
- 409 response includes error property.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "implement optimistic locking for patch" "npm run test:concurrent"

Goal:
Handle two bulk jobs with overlapping order IDs safely.

Implementation notes:
- Use row locks, SKIP LOCKED, idempotency, or atomic update rules.
- Goal is no corrupt/double-processed state.

Acceptance criteria:
- Both overlapping jobs complete.
- Combined completed+failed covers unique IDs.
- Each job reports its own total.
- No order status becomes invalid.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "prevent unsafe overlapping bulk processing" "npm run test:concurrent"

Goal:
GET requests during bulk operations must return valid, consistent data.

Implementation notes:
- Use normal committed transactions.
- Avoid partial writes that break aggregates.

Acceptance criteria:
- GET /api/orders/stats during bulk returns total_orders=50000.
- GET /api/orders/:id during bulk returns valid status.
- No corrupt/null required fields.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "ensure read consistency during bulk" "npm run test:concurrent"

Goal:
Support concurrent PATCH, bulk, and GET load.

Implementation notes:
- Tune DB pool size if necessary.
- Ensure transactions do not deadlock under test load.

Acceptance criteria:
- 10 concurrent PATCH to different orders succeed.
- 5 non-overlapping bulk actions complete.
- 50 concurrent GET /api/orders return 200 quickly.
- Commit and push the slice.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "pass concurrency stress tests" "npm run test:concurrent"

Goal:
Expose realtime event stream using Server-Sent Events at /api/events.

Implementation notes:
- SSE is simpler than WebSocket and tests auto-detect either.
- Use data: JSON lines.

Acceptance criteria:
- Client can connect with Accept: text/event-stream.
- Connection stays open.
- Disconnect cleanup works.
- Events are JSON objects with type and data.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "implement /api/events sse stream" "npm run test:realtime"

Goal:
Emit order_updated whenever an order status changes.

Implementation notes:
- Wire PATCH and bulk status updates to EventPublisher.

Acceptance criteria:
- Event arrives within 5s.
- Event type is order_updated.
- Data includes id, old_status, new_status, updated_at.
- Multiple clients receive broadcast events.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "broadcast order_updated events" "npm run test:realtime"

Goal:
Allow clients to connect to /api/events?supplier_id=sup_042 and receive only that supplier's events.

Implementation notes:
- Events need supplier_id available at publish time or lookup.

Acceptance criteria:
- Filtered client receives matching supplier events.
- Filtered client does not receive unrelated supplier events.
- Unfiltered clients still receive all events.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "support supplier_id filtered subscriptions" "npm run test:realtime"

Goal:
Emit realtime event when a bulk job completes.

Implementation notes:
- Reuse bulk_completed hook from bulk slice.

Acceptance criteria:
- Event type is bulk_completed.
- Data includes jobId.
- Event arrives within test timeout.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "broadcast bulk_completed events" "npm run test:realtime"

Goal:
Run and fix the full realtime test suite.

Implementation notes:
- Keep prior suites passing.

Acceptance criteria:
- All realtime tests pass.
- Commit and push the slice.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "pass realtime suite" "npm run test:realtime"

Goal:
Validate API bodies and query params to prevent invalid state and obvious injection payloads.

Implementation notes:
- Centralize validation if possible.
- Return JSON error responses.

Acceptance criteria:
- PATCH with SQL injection-like status returns 400.
- Invalid status returns 400.
- Bulk with 10001 IDs returns 400.
- limit=-1 returns 400 or sensible clamped limit <=100.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "harden input validation" "npm run test:security"

Goal:
Ensure every user value uses parameterized queries and every dynamic SQL part is allowlisted.

Implementation notes:
- Audit repositories and query builders.
- Sort fields/order direction must never be directly interpolated from user input.

Acceptance criteria:
- No user values interpolated into SQL.
- Sort field allowlist exists.
- Order direction allowlist exists.
- Security tests pass.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "audit sql injection protection" "npm run test:security"

Goal:
Allow notes/reason strings containing script-like content without rendering raw HTML or changing content type.

Implementation notes:
- Backend should return JSON.
- Frontend should render notes as text only.

Acceptance criteria:
- PATCH notes with script tag returns JSON.

Goal:

Implementation notes:

Acceptance criteria:

- GET /api/orders?status=pending&sort=created_at p95 < 200ms.
- GET /api/orders?search=hydraulic p95 < 300ms.

Commit after:
npm run git:slice -- "optimize list and search endpoints" "npm run test:perf"

- GET /api/orders/anomalies p95 < 1000ms.
